postData.contentHtml:
In a recent viral sensation, former President Donald Trump has utilized advanced AI video generation to depict himself as a medical professional, humorously 'diagnosing' his critics with 'Trump Derangement Syndrome.' The video, shared across social media platforms, shows an AI-generated Trump advising patients to switch off 'fake news' and even suggests Diet Coke as a remedy for anxiety related to the fictional ailment.

This particular video is part of a broader series of AI-generated content from Trump's camp, which has also featured other prominent figures. Celebrities such as Rosie O'Donnell, Julia Roberts, and Whoopi Goldberg have been mocked in similar AI-created clips, ostensibly 'suffering' from the same 'syndrome.'

The move has ignited a fresh wave of discussion online. While social media sentiment largely appears positive among his supporters, the videos have also drawn sharp criticism. Notably, actress Rosie O'Donnell has publicly responded to Trump's AI depiction with her own critiques, adding another layer to the ongoing public discourse surrounding the use of AI in political satire and communication.

This deployment of AI in political messaging highlights both the innovative potential and the contentious nature of new technologies in public discourse, pushing boundaries on how political figures interact with their audience and critics.
postData.title: AI Goes Viral: Donald Trump's 'Derangement Syndrome' Video Sparks Debate
postData.summary: Donald Trump has leveraged AI technology to create and disseminate a video portraying himself as a doctor offering a 'cure' for 'Trump Derangement Syndrome,' leading to mixed reactions and celebrity responses.

sources.title: Donald Trump posts AI video of himself treating critics for 'derangement syndrome'
Trump posts AI video of Rosie O'Donnell, more celebs claiming to have derangement syndrome
Rosie O'Donnell Offers A Brutal Diagnosis For 'Doctor' Trump After Bonkers AI Video
Celebrities with 'Trump Derangement Syndrome' mocked as 'Dr. Trump' shares cure in AI video
'Doctor' Trump Posts Bonkers AI Slop Video Mocking His 'So Far Gone' Celebrity Critics
